import io
import string
import list

fn remove_at(_ xs: List<Int>, _ target_idx: Int) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  while i < xs.len()
    if i != target_idx
      match xs.get(i)
      when Some(v)
        result = list.push(result, v)
      when None
        # skip
      end
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

fn main() -> Result<Unit, String>
  let line1 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end

  let line2 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end

  let tokens = string.split_whitespace(line1)

  mut nums: List<Int> = []
  mut ti = 0
  while ti < tokens.len()
    match tokens.get(ti)
    when Some(tok)
      match string.parse_int(tok)
      when Some(n)
        nums = list.push(nums, n)
      when None
        # skip non-integer tokens
      end
    when None
      # skip
    end
    ti = ti + 1
  end

  let k_str = string.trim(line2)
  mut k = 0
  match string.parse_int(k_str)
  when Some(n)
    k = n
  when None
    k = 0
  end

  let total = nums.len()
  let take = if k < total
    k
  else
    total
  end

  mut remaining = nums
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut taken = 0

  while taken < take and remaining.len() > 0
    match list.max(remaining)
    when Some(max_val)
      result = list.push(result, max_val)
      mut remove_idx = 0
      match list.index_of(remaining, max_val)
      when Some(i)
        remove_idx = i
      when None
        # max_val came from list.max so index_of must succeed
      end
      remaining = remove_at(remaining, remove_idx)
      taken = taken + 1
    when None
      taken = take
    end
  end

  mut output = ""
  mut ri = 0
  while ri < result.len()
    match result.get(ri)
    when Some(v)
      if ri == 0
        output = "#{v}"
      else
        output = "#{output} #{v}"
      end
    when None
      # skip
    end
    ri = ri + 1
  end

  println(output)
  Ok(())
end
